critic_raw:
Issue 1: Metric-to-claim mismatch. The board's claim is about nighttime braking safety. The evidence is per-frame COCO-style mAP. These are not the same outcome measure.

Issue 2: The planner requires temporal persistence — 400ms of continuous detection — at minimum stopping distance. Per-frame metrics do not measure this. A detector that produces fragmented or unstable detections across frames, even with good per-frame overlap, may fail the planner's trigger criterion.

Issue 3: 38ms inference time versus 24ms on the production SoC. This is not captured in mAP. A 58% latency increase could reduce effective lead time at a given speed even if box quality improves.
defender_raw:
The mAP result is on a Euro NCAP-compliant nighttime validation set using identical camera hardware and annotation conventions. This is a rigorous detector evaluation. The 19% mAP improvement distributed broadly across box sizes is strong evidence that the detector is genuinely better at the detection task.

The defender concedes Issues 1 and 2 at a framing level: per-frame mAP is not the same as braking safety evidence. The correct conclusion is that the detector result is a promising indicator that merits planner-in-the-loop validation, not that the board can cite it as a safety advancement without further testing.

On latency: systems engineering stated 38ms remains within the ECU budget. The latency concern is worth investigating in planner-in-the-loop testing, but it is not established as problematic from the current data.
adjudication_raw: Critic and defender converge quickly. The detector quality result is valid; the safety claim requires planner-in-the-loop validation. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: metric mismatch — per-frame mAP vs nighttime braking safety claim
Issue 2: COCO per-frame mAP does not measure 400ms temporal persistence
Issue 3: 38ms vs 24ms latency on production SoC not in headline metric

empirical_test.condition: Planner-in-the-loop closed-track testing measuring nighttime braking success rate and stopping margin
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: No improvement or worse nighttime braking outcomes because temporal persistence and latency dominate
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Significant improvement in nighttime braking success and stopping margin despite slower runtime
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Detector helps some nighttime scenarios but trades off against others
